name: Blood Pressure Measurement Codes
description: CPT codes for blood pressure measurement and screening services. Used in BP control measures to identify encounters where BP was measured. Note: actual BP values come from vital sign data, not procedure codes alone.
source: Public CMS/clinical references — placeholder for official NCQA value sets

codes.description: Collection and interpretation of physiologic data digitally stored and/or transmitted by the patient
Most recent systolic blood pressure less than 130 mm Hg
Most recent systolic blood pressure 130-139 mm Hg
Most recent systolic blood pressure greater than or equal to 140 mm Hg
Most recent diastolic blood pressure less than 80 mm Hg
Most recent diastolic blood pressure 80-89 mm Hg
Most recent diastolic blood pressure greater than or equal to 90 mm Hg
Ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, utilizing a system such as magnetic tape and/or computer disk, 24 hours or longer; including recording, scanning analysis, interpretation and report
Ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, utilizing a system such as magnetic tape and/or computer disk, 24 hours or longer; recording only
Ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, utilizing a system such as magnetic tape and/or computer disk, 24 hours or longer; scanning analysis with report
Ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, physician review with interpretation and report
